# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

{
  "$schema": "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#",
  "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
  "resources": [
    {
      "type": "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults",
      "apiVersion": "2021-04-01-preview",
      "name": "[parameters('keyVaultName')]",
      "location": "[parameters('location')]",
      "properties": {
        "sku": {
          "family": "A",
          "name": "standard"
        },
        "tenantId": "[subscription().tenantId]",
        "accessPolicies": [
          {
            "tenantId": "[subscription().tenantId]",
            "objectId": "[parameters('app1ObjectId')]",
            "permissions": {
              "secrets": [
                "get",
                "list"
              ]
            }
          },
          {
            "tenantId": "[subscription().tenantId]",
            "objectId": "[parameters('app2ObjectId')]",
            "permissions": {
              "secrets": [
                "get",
                "list"
              ]
            }
          },
          {
            "tenantId": "[subscription().tenantId]",
            "objectId": "[parameters('servicePrincipalObjectId')]",
            "permissions": {
              "secrets": [
                "get",
                "list"
              ]
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ],
  "parameters": {
    "keyVaultName": {
      "type": "string",
      "metadata": {
        "description": "Name of the Key Vault."
      }
    },
    "location": {
      "type": "string",
      "defaultValue": "[resourceGroup().location]",
      "metadata": {
        "description": "Location for the Key Vault."
      }
    },
    "app1ObjectId": {
      "type": "string",
      "metadata": {
        "description": "Object ID of the first application."
      }
    },
    "app2ObjectId": {
      "type": "string",
      "metadata": {
        "description": "Object ID of the second application."
      }
    },
    "servicePrincipalObjectId": {
      "type": "string",
      "metadata": {
        "description": "Object ID of the service principal."
      }
    }
  }
}
